title: Fixing a React Memoized Component with Object Equality Issue

statement.markdown: You are given a memoized React component that uses the `useMemo` hook to optimize performance. However, the component is experiencing re-renders when the object passed as a dependency changes, even if the object itself doesn't change. Your task is to fix the bug by making the `useMemo` hook consider the object's equality instead of its reference.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an object with string properties.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the same object with its properties memoized.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the object's properties change.

examples.explanation: The object's properties are memoized, and the component shouldn't re-render when the object reference changes.
The object's properties change, and the component should re-render with the new object.

hints.text: Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare object properties.

editorial.title: Fixing the Memoized Component
editorial.intuition: The `useMemo` hook should consider the object's equality instead of its reference.
editorial.bruteForce: Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare object properties.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare object properties and make the `useMemo` hook consider the object's equality.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component will only re-render when the object's properties change.
